VaccineValidateR 1.0.0
  • Reference
  • Articles

Build CVX Mapping List

Source: R/utils.R
build_cvx_map.Rd

Utility function to create a structured list of CVX code groups for each antigen of interest. This function is called automatically at package load to populate the global constant CVX.

Usage

build_cvx_map(lim_cvx_map = NULL)

Arguments

lim_cvx_map

Optional data.table containing local immunization identifiers (LIM) mapped to CVX codes. Must include at least PRODUCT and CVX.

Value

A named list of CVX code vectors (cvx), each corresponding to an antigen or grouped antigen set (e.g., HIB, POLIO, COVID, LIVE_NON_ENTERAL).

Details

Most users will not need to call this directly. To refresh the global mapping (e.g., with a custom LIM to CVX file), use update_cvx_map instead.

The mapping combines reference CVX-antigen definitions bundled in extdata/ with an optional local LIM to CVX mapping provided by the user. The result is assigned to CVX when the package loads, making the mapping available globally.

See also

cvx, update_cvx_map, supported_antigens

Examples

# Normally not needed, CVX is available after library load:
CVX$HIB
#> Error: object 'CVX' not found

# Rebuild manually (not usually recommended)
custom_map <- build_cvx_map()
custom_map$POLIO
#>  [1]    2   10   89  110  120  130  130  132  146  160  178  179  180  182  324
#> [16] 1002 1003

# Supported way to refresh the global mapping
update_cvx_map()
#> Error in assign("cvx", new_map, envir = parent.env(environment())): cannot change value of locked binding for 'cvx'
